ProcessingMethod.description:
Method used to detect grid coordinates in the web/API pipeline.

NOTE: This is distinct from core.queue_models.ProcessingMethod, which
defines methods for the CLI/GUI pipeline (FIXED_GRID, ANCHOR_DETECTION, MANUAL).
The web pipeline uses OCR_ANCHORED and LINE_BASED, which map to the DI-based
ScreenshotProcessingService in core/screenshot_processing.py.
Do not attempt to unify these two enums — they serve different pipelines.
ProcessingMethod.title: ProcessingMethod

ProcessingResultResponse.description:
Result of processing/reprocessing a screenshot.

Contains all data needed by the frontend to display results:
- Extracted data (title, total, hourly values)
- Grid coordinates (for drawing the overlay)
- Processing metadata (method, confidence, issues)

info.description: Multi-user platform for processing iPhone screen time and battery screenshots
info.title: iOS Screenshot Processing API

get.description: Root endpoint providing API information.

post.description:
Queue screenshots for reprocessing via Celery workers.

This will reprocess all screen_time screenshots in the specified group
(or all groups if not specified) using the updated processing code.
Uses Celery for background processing to avoid blocking the API.

Admin only.

parameters.description: Max pixels to shift grid for optimization
parameters.name: max_shift

schema.description: Max pixels to shift grid for optimization
schema.title: Max Shift

get.description: Get the status of a bulk reprocess operation. Admin only.

parameters.description: Group ID to check status for
parameters.name: group_id

schema.description: Group ID to check status for
schema.title: Group Id
Response Get Bulk Reprocess Status Api V1 Admin Bulk Reprocess Status Get

post.description:
Delete orphaned database entries that reference non-existent screenshots.
Admin only.

Delete UserQueueState rows for screenshots that are completed or deleted.

These stale entries accumulate over time and are no longer needed once the
associated screenshot has been fully processed or soft-deleted.

Admin only.

delete.description:
Delete a group and all its screenshots (hard delete).
This is a destructive operation that permanently removes:
- All screenshots in the group
- All annotations for those screenshots
- The group itself

Admin only.

get.description:
Find orphaned database entries that reference non-existent screenshots.
Admin only.

post.description:
Recalculate OCR totals for screen_time screenshots that are missing extracted_total.
This runs OCR on the original image to extract the total usage time.

Admin only.

Reset test data for e2e testing.
Clears user queue states and annotations to allow fresh test runs.
Admin only.

Retry screenshots stuck in PENDING or PROCESSING status.

This endpoint:
1. Optionally marks all PROCESSING screenshots as FAILED (they're stuck/orphaned)
2. Requeues all PENDING screenshots to Celery for processing

Use this when screenshots are stuck and not being processed by Celery workers.
Admin only.

parameters.description: Mark screenshots stuck in PROCESSING as FAILED before requeuing PENDING ones
parameters.name: mark_processing_as_failed

schema.description: Mark screenshots stuck in PROCESSING as FAILED before requeuing PENDING ones
schema.title: Mark Processing As Failed

get.description:
Get all users with their annotation statistics. Admin only.

Uses a single query with LEFT JOIN to avoid N+1 problem.

schema.title: Response Get All Users Api V1 Admin Users Get

put.description: Update user status or role. Admin only.

post.description:
Create or update an annotation (upsert).
If user already has an annotation for this screenshot, update it.
Otherwise, create a new one.

schema.title: Response Get User Annotation History Api V1 Annotations History Get

post.description:
Login with username and optional password.

Auto-creates user on first login. Returns user info.

get.description:
Get current user info.

Requires X-Username header (and X-Site-Password if configured).

post.description:
Login with username and optional password, get session cookie.

Creates a session and sets an HTTP-only secure cookie.
Frontend should redirect here if no valid session exists.

Logout and clear session cookie.

Deletes the session from storage and clears the cookie.

schema.title: Response Session Logout Api V1 Auth Session Logout Post

get.description:
Get current session info.

Returns the username from the current session.
Requires valid session (enforced by middleware).

schema.title: Response Session Me Api V1 Auth Session Me Get

get.description:
Check if authentication is required.

Public endpoint - frontend uses this to determine if login is needed.

post.description:
Verify site password (stateless check).

Does not create a session - frontend should store password
and send via X-Site-Password header on subsequent requests.

get.description:
Get all groups with verification tier breakdown.

Returns groups with counts for:
- single_verified: Verified by exactly 1 user
- agreed: 2+ users verified, all values match exactly
- disputed: 2+ users verified, any difference exists

schema.title: Response Get Groups With Verification Tiers Api V1 Consensus Groups Get

get.description: Get screenshots in a specific verification tier for a group.

schema.title: Response Get Screenshots By Tier Api V1 Consensus Groups  Group Id  Screenshots Get

get.description:
Get comparison data for a screenshot with multiple verifiers.
Shows each verifier's annotation and highlights differences.

post.description:
Resolve a dispute by setting the final agreed-upon values.

Requires admin role. Updates the screenshot's extracted_* fields directly
(like a normal GUI edit), so changes are immediately visible everywhere.
Original OCR values are preserved in resolved_* fields for audit/rollback.

get.description: Get summary statistics for consensus analysis.
Get consensus analysis for a specific screenshot.

post.description: Recalculate consensus for a specific screenshot.

schema.title: Response List Screenshots Api V1 Screenshots  Get
Response Get Disputed Screenshots Api V1 Screenshots Disputed Get

get.description:
Export consensus data as CSV.

Returns CSV with screenshot info, consensus values, and hourly data.
Available to all authenticated users.

Filters:
- group_id: Filter by group
- verified_only: Only include screenshots that have been verified by at least one user
- has_annotations: Only include screenshots with at least one annotation
- processing_status: Filter by OCR processing status (completed, failed, pending, skipped)

parameters.description: Only export screenshots verified by at least one user
parameters.name: verified_only

schema.description: Only export screenshots verified by at least one user
schema.title: Verified Only

parameters.description: Only export screenshots with at least one annotation
parameters.name: has_annotations

schema.description: Only export screenshots with at least one annotation
schema.title: Has Annotations

parameters.description: Filter by processing status (completed, failed, etc.)
parameters.name: processing_status

schema.description: Filter by processing status (completed, failed, etc.)
schema.title: Processing Status

get.description: Export consensus data as JSON.

Return the canonical export column headers.

This endpoint is the single source of truth for export column names.
WASM/Tauri clients use this to build client-side CSV exports with
identical column schema to the server CSV export.

schema.title: Response Get Export Column Schema Api V1 Screenshots Export Schema Get

get.description:
List all groups with screenshot counts by processing_status.

Results are cached in-memory with a configurable TTL (default 10s).

schema.title: Response List Groups Api V1 Screenshots Groups Get

List screenshots with comprehensive filtering and pagination.

schema.title: Verified By Me

schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Filter by processing status (pending, completed, failed, skipped)

delete.description: Remove a text string from the PHI whitelist.

get.description: Return the current PHI text whitelist.

post.description: Add a text string to the PHI whitelist so it is never flagged again.
Queue preprocessing tasks for multiple screenshots in a group.
Run cropping in background with chunked commits for live progress.
Run device detection in background with chunked commits for live progress.
Queue batch OCR processing (grid detection + title/total + hourly extraction).
Queue PHI detection for a batch of screenshots.

Revoke queued/running PHI detection tasks and reset affected screenshots to pending.

Accepts task_ids (from the original dispatch response) to revoke specific tasks.
Also resets any screenshots in the group that are still marked 'running' back to
'pending' so they can be re-queued.
Queue PHI redaction for a batch of screenshots.

Reset a stage to 'pending' for all completed/failed screenshots in a group.

Also invalidates all downstream stages. This allows re-running a stage
that has already completed.

get.description: Get per-stage counts for a group's preprocessing pipeline.

Get screenshot statistics using consolidated queries.

Results are cached in-memory with a configurable TTL (default 10s,
set via STATS_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS env var) to avoid 6+ COUNT queries
on every page load.

post.description:
Upload a screenshot with base64-encoded image data.

Features:
- Base64-encoded PNG/JPEG images (max 100 MB)
- Auto-detection of device type from image dimensions
- SHA256 checksum verification (optional)
- Idempotency key for safe retries
- Callback URL for webhook notifications when processing completes
- Groups auto-created if they don't exist
- Duplicate detection by file hash

Rate limit: 180 requests/minute

Headers:
    X-API-Key: API key for authorization

Returns:
    Extended metadata including file path, dimensions, and processing status

Upload multiple screenshots in a single request.

Features:
- Upload up to 60 screenshots per batch
- All screenshots share the same group_id and image_type
- Individual SHA256 checksum verification per image
- Partial success handling - failed items don't block successful ones
- Callback URL for webhook notification when batch processing completes

Rate limit: 30 batches/minute (separate from single upload limit)

Headers:
    X-API-Key: API key for authorization

Returns:
    Summary of batch results with individual item status

Upload screenshots from browser with multipart/form-data.

Uses X-Username auth (CurrentUser), NOT X-API-Key.
Accepts metadata as a JSON string plus one or more image files.
Does not queue Celery tasks — user triggers stages manually.
Apply PHI redaction to confirmed regions.

get.description: Serve screenshot image file. No auth required — tags can't send X-Username headers. The UI itself is auth-gated; images are not sensitive data.

post.description: Manually invalidate downstream stages for a screenshot.

Apply a manual crop to a screenshot.

Crops the original image and records an event in the preprocessing log.
Automatically invalidates phi_detection and phi_redaction downstream.
CPU-bound OpenCV work runs in a thread to avoid blocking the event loop.

get.description:
Get a screenshot with navigation context within filtered results.
Returns the current, next, or previous screenshot based on direction.

parameters.description: Filter by current user's verification (True=verified by me, False=not verified by me)
parameters.name: verified_by_me

schema.description: Filter by current user's verification (True=verified by me, False=not verified by me)
schema.title: Verified By Me

parameters.description: Filter for screenshots verified by others but not current user (True only)
parameters.name: verified_by_others

schema.description: Filter for screenshots verified by others but not current user (True only)
schema.title: Verified By Others

get.description: Serve the immutable original image (base_file_path) for crop editing.
Get current PHI regions from the phi_detection event.

put.description: Save manually-adjusted PHI regions. Invalidates phi_redaction downstream.

post.description: Queue preprocessing task for a single screenshot.

get.description: Get preprocessing details for a screenshot from its processing_metadata.
Get the full preprocessing event log for a screenshot.

post.description:
Recalculate the OCR total for a specific screenshot.
Re-runs OCR extraction on the original image to get the total usage time.

Reprocess a screenshot with optional grid coordinates and processing method.

Processing methods:
- If grid coordinates are provided, uses "manual" method
- If processing_method="line_based", uses visual line detection (no OCR for grid)
- Otherwise, uses "ocr_anchored" method (finds "12 AM" and "60" text anchors)
Restore a soft-deleted screenshot to its previous processing_status.

Skip a screenshot globally by setting processing_status to 'skipped'.
This moves it to the skipped category visible on the homepage.
Soft delete a screenshot by setting processing_status to DELETED.

get.description:
Serve the output image of a specific preprocessing stage.

Walks the event log to find the current event for the given stage and
serves its output_file.  Falls back to the stage's input_file, then to
base_file_path.

post.description: Unskip a screenshot by restoring processing_status to 'completed'.

delete.description: Remove verification mark from a screenshot for the current user.

post.description:
Mark a screenshot as verified by the current user.
This adds the user's ID to the verified_by_user_ids list without removing the screenshot from the queue.
Optionally saves the current grid coordinates to freeze them at verification time.

get.description:
Comprehensive health check endpoint.

Returns health status including database connectivity.
Set include_celery=true to also check Celery worker availability.
